north_star_verdict.headline: GOAA keeps meeting with Zycus (2 calls, 5 named attendees, a rescheduled but not cancelled demo) but the 27 Aug 2026 close is unrealistic for a public-sector Qualified-stage deal with no discovery run, no Economic Buyer, and no decision process confirmed.

flags: Run full MEDDPICC discovery on 1 Jul: map the Economic Buyer (likely Kelly Loll VP Procurement or Lance Lyttle CEO), confirm the approval chain and budget threshold, surface decision criteria (must-haves, scoring rubric, RFP vs sole-source), quantify the pain (spend leakage, cycle time, manual processes), and ask what happens if they do nothing. Capture answers in writing and log them in Salesforce Business Requirements and Value to Customer fields.

Activate AI differentiation early (1 Jul demo): position Merlin Intake (AI request triage, deflection, NLP) + ML iAnalyze (spend intelligence, supplier risk, contract analytics) as the modern, AI-native stack that beats Amazon Business's shallow marketplace analytics and Oracle's legacy complexity. Emphasize time-to-value, ease-of-use, and ROI for a mid-market public-sector buyer. Make AI the centerpiece, not a feature.
No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped. Cannot validate budget, approval authority, or timeline feasibility until the 1 Jul call surfaces the buying committee and Grace adds contact roles.

Public-sector procurement complexity is the single largest risk to the 27 Aug close date. Greater Orlando Aviation Authority is a public entity; deals of this scope often require formal RFP, vendor registration, board approval, and InfoSec/legal review, which can add 3-6 months. On the 01 Jul call, ask explicitly: Is this a sole-source or competitive bid? What approvals are required? What is the realistic timeline from vendor selection to signature? If a formal RFP or board approval is required, recalibrate the close date to Q4 2026 immediately after the call.

Quantify the Spend Analysis pain on the 01 Jul call. Ask Kelly or Cheryl to quantify current manual effort, supplier fragmentation cost, compliance risk, or savings opportunity. Co-build a simple ROI model (e.g. 15% savings on $XM addressable spend, Y hours/week freed) the Economic Buyer can defend to Finance or the board. Without a quantified business case the investment cannot be approved.

The 01 Jul discovery/demo is the single highest-leverage event in this deal's lifecycle so far. Use it to: (1) quantify the Spend Analysis and eProcurement pain with cost/FTE/cycle-time numbers, (2) map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker (ask who approves a $100k software purchase), (3) extract decision criteria and build the demo around them, (4) validate the procurement process (sole-source vs RFP, board approval, legal/InfoSec), (5) assess and develop a champion (who internally is most excited, who has CFO/CEO access), (6) capture competitive sentiment on Amazon Business and Oracle and position Zycus AI differentiation. Record detailed notes and update the opportunity immediately after the call.

Position AI differentiation early against Amazon Business and Oracle. Merlin Intake's agentic workflow automation is a capability neither competitor can match. Make it the Phase 2 centerpiece on the 21 Jul 2026 demo, and anchor the business case to AI-driven savings (faster approvals, reduced maverick spend, intelligent supplier recommendations) to justify platform pricing vs Amazon Business's transaction fees.
Use the Gartner/Forrester reports promised on 16 Jun 2026 as an early credibility anchor, since public-sector cycles are slow and trust built now compounds later.

Lead the 21 Jul 2026 demo with a quantified spend-analysis and eProcurement ROI story (ML iAnalyze, Merlin Intake) since GOAA's only stated interest so far, 'active project for Spend Analysis, interest in eProc,' is still unquantified.
